vitals.date_specific: Granfondo/Mediofondo road race: Sunday, June 7, 2026 at 08:00 (festival runs June 5-7)
vitals.entry_fee: EUR60 licensed / EUR70 unlicensed (standard tier); Gold entry EUR99/EUR109

climate.description: Early June in the Brenta Dolomites/Trentino Alps — cool mornings, potential for afternoon mountain storms and cold at altitude.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 61. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 59. NOT re-scored this wave despite the location/identity correction (Trento -> Pinzolo) -- dimensions left untouched pending a full re-review; flagging that field_depth/prestige/scenic_experience were originally set against a different (Trento city, UCI Worlds-adjacent) profile and may not reflect the actual Pinzolo/Top Dolomites event.

course_description.character: Start/finish in Pinzolo, in the Brenta Dolomites (Trentino), not in Trento city as this profile previously said. The 2026 festival ran June 5-7 with two competitive road distances on Sunday, June 7: a 111 km Granfondo (2,780 m official route-page elevation) and a 77 km Mediofondo (1,930 m). The organizer's own post-event report for 2026 gives slightly lower totals (2,600 m and 1,650 m) -- the two official sources don't agree, so treat the elevation figures as approximate rather than survey-exact. There is no short/entry-level course. Francesco Moser is the event's official ambassador, which is almost certainly the source of the informal 'Granfondo Moser' label -- but that is not this event's actual name, and it is not held in Trento city.
course_description.signature_challenge: The climbing through the Brenta Dolomites on the 111 km Granfondo route -- roughly 2,700-2,800 m of gain.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Event is based in Pinzolo, Val Rendena (Trentino) -- not Trento city or Madonna di Campiglio, though Campiglio is nearby in the same valley and may offer additional lodging options.

history.origin_story: Launched in 2018 as Granfondo Top Dolomites, based in Pinzolo. Francesco Moser serves as the event's official ambassador -- likely the origin of the informal 'Granfondo Moser' shorthand this profile previously used as its actual name. Not held in 2020 (per organizer history); 2026 was its eighth edition.

biased_opinion.summary: There is no gran fondo officially called 'Granfondo Moser.' That label conflates at least three separate, real Trentino events connected to Francesco Moser: La Francesco Moser, a non-competitive cicloturistica based in Trento city running since roughly 1989 (60 km, Trento-Val di Cembra-Trento); La Moserissima, a vintage/ciclostorica ride launched in 2015; and Granfondo Top Dolomites, the current competitive gran fondo based in Pinzolo, for which Moser serves as official ambassador. This profile's prior course description and video content described the 2022 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships course held in Trento city -- a one-off world championship, not an annual 'Granfondo Moser,' and not the same event as Top Dolomites. We've corrected the identity to Granfondo Top Dolomites, the closest real, currently active competitive gran fondo carrying a Moser association, and moved the location to where it's actually held: Pinzolo, not Trento.
strengths: A currently active competitive gran fondo -- eighth edition in 2026, with Francesco Moser as official ambassador
1,400+ riders from 20 countries in the 2026 edition
Mountain terrain in the Brenta Dolomites, and an FCI (Italian Cycling Federation)-listed race, not just an organizer's self-description
weaknesses: The name 'Granfondo Moser' is not this event's official name anywhere, and it invites real confusion with at least three distinct Trentino events -- this profile previously mixed in footage from an unrelated 2022 UCI World Championship course in Trento city
Based in Pinzolo, not Trento city -- riders searching for a 'Trento' gran fondo under this name will land in the wrong valley
Skipped in 2020 (COVID-era gap) -- not an uninterrupted annual event
The organizer's two official elevation sources for 2026 disagree with each other (2,780 m vs. 2,600 m on the long course, 1,930 m vs. 1,650 m on the medium) -- there's no single authoritative number
No shorter competitive Sunday race distance -- the festival's only easier option (June 5's 'Brenta Bike Experience') is a non-competitive touring ride, not a race
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want a real, competitive Moser-associated gran fondo, this is Granfondo Top Dolomites in Pinzolo -- not a Trento city event, and not the same thing as the non-competitive La Francesco Moser ride or the vintage La Moserissima. Book accordingly, and expect the organizer's own elevation figures to disagree with themselves by a couple hundred meters.

final_verdict.one_liner: Pinzolo's Brenta Dolomites gran fondo carries a real Moser tie-in, but it isn't the Trento race the old name implied.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you want genuine competitive racing in the Brenta Dolomites with FCI classification and don't mind the Trentino mountains rather than Trento city itself. Skip it if you were expecting a Trento-based ride -- that's La Francesco Moser (casual cicloturistica) or La Moserissima (vintage), both different events.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the casual, Trento-based Moser connection: La Francesco Moser cicloturistica (60 km, non-competitive). For vintage/retro: La Moserissima. For a bigger competitive Dolomites test at similar distance: Maratona dles Dolomites or Sportful Dolomiti Race.

catalog_flags.status_note: Identity-corrected this wave (see eligibility.notes): the profile was named/vitaled as 'Granfondo Moser' in Trento with course content actually describing the 2022 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships (a one-off championship, also Trento city). No event is officially named 'Granfondo Moser' -- the name conflates La Francesco Moser (casual Trento cicloturistica), La Moserissima (vintage ride), and Granfondo Top Dolomites (the current competitive gran fondo, Pinzolo, Moser as ambassador). Corrected name/location/vitals to Granfondo Top Dolomites (Pinzolo) as the closest real, active, Moser-associated competitive gran fondo. Flagging for a human call: fondo_rating dimensions were never re-scored against the corrected (Pinzolo, not Trento) identity and may not fit -- recommend a full re-score pass rather than trusting the inherited numbers.

eligibility.notes: IDENTITY CORRECTION (2026-07-24): 'Granfondo Moser' is not an official event name. This profile previously combined a Trento location/vitals with course-preview footage of the 2022 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships (also Trento city, a one-off championship, not an annual race). Corrected to Granfondo Top Dolomites, the real, currently active competitive gran fondo based in Pinzolo (not Trento) for which Francesco Moser serves as official ambassador -- confirmed held June 7, 2026 (eighth edition, 1,400+ riders from 20 countries) via the organizer's own 2026 post-event report. Distinct, separate real events also exist under the Moser name: La Francesco Moser (60km non-competitive cicloturistica, Trento city) and La Moserissima (vintage/ciclostorica ride). Neither is this profile's subject.
